title: 200 Francs - Eiffel (type 1995)

obverse.description:
Red and dark maroon print on predominantly red and multicolour underprint; serial number black.
Portrait of the famous French builder Gustave Eiffel (1832 –1923) in front of the Garabit viaduct designed by him; basically; the colorful detail of a frame evokes the Eiffel Tower. Below is a stylized representation of the Eiffel Tower.
obverse.lettering:
200 Deux Cents Francs
Gustave Eiffel
1832-1923
200
Banque de France
obverse.lettering_translation:
Bank of France, Two Hundred Francs
Gustave Eiffel

reverse.description: A view of the base of Eiffel Tower and the Champ-de-Mars during the Universal Exhibition of 1889; in the background, the dome of the Palais des Beaux-Arts; stylized representation of the Eiffel Tower.
reverse.lettering:
Banque de France
200
200 Deux Cents Francs
LA CONTREFAÇON OU LA FALSIFICATION DES BILLETS DE BANQUE ET LA MISE EN CIRCULATION DES BILLETS CONTREFAITS OU FALSIFIÉS SONT PUNIS PAR LES ARTICLES 442-1 et 442-2 DU CODE PÉNAL DE PEINES ALLANT JUSQU'À TRENTE ANS DE RÉCLUSION CRIMINELLE ET TROIS MILLIONS DE FRANCS D'AMENDE.
reverse.lettering_translation:
Bank of France
Two Hundred Francs
Counterfeiting or falsification of banknotes and the release of counterfeit and falsified notes are punishable by articles 442-1 and 442-2 or the penal code of sentences of up to 30 years of criminal reclusion and a 3 million franc fine.

comments:
Two combinations of signatories :
 a) Didier Bruneel – Jean Bonnardin – Claude Vigier (1995 et 1996)

 b, c) Didier Bruneel – Jean Bonnardin – Yves Barroux (1996 et 1997) et (1999)

UV activity:

© gyoschak

 

The footprint of the Eiffel Tower at the top-left corner on the obverse side is printed in colour-shifting ink, changes colours between two different shades of green and turquoise:

© Kiet Tran

 

Security thread:

Solid security thread to the right of the larger foil strip, only visible under backlight. No inscription on the security thread.

© Kiet Tran
